events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are asked: "Compliance check: Overcurrent device selection & coordination (industrial site)". We need to evaluate based on the scenario. The scenario: "Closeout photos for Overcurrent device selection & coordination show the installed work following the referenced detail. Clearances, labels, supports, and terminations shown for this element are in place, and the inspection card has no open correction note. Reference material on hand: NEC 240, 210.8, 210.12, 110.14(C) termination temp ratings."

The scenario indicates that closeout photos show the installed work following the referenced detail. Clearances, labels, supports, terminations are in place. Inspection card has no open correction note. So everything seems compliant. The task says: "decide whether the work complies with the applicable code, specification, or manufacturer requirements". Based on the description, it looks compliant. So decision likely "pass". Risk low? Work stage "rough-complete" as given: "Stage of the work when observed: rough-complete". Component conditions: none flagged, so empty list. Percent complete: probably high, maybe 100% for this element? The scenario says "roughed in — installed or assembled, but not yet closed out". So it's not fully complete, maybe 90%? But the instruction: percent_complete is your numeric estimate of overall progress. The work stage is rough-complete, so maybe 90%? I'll put 90. Value: no numeric reading asked. Sound source etc. not asked, set to null or empty string? They say "when asked", so I can leave default. Confidence: high? I'll put 0.95. Event time and rate: not relevant, put 0. Order: not asked, empty. Workable: not asked, empty list. Findings: none, so empty list. Actions: none needed, empty. Rationale: explain. References: NEC sections maybe. I'll say "NEC 240, 110.14(C)" because termination temp ratings are okay. Also note that no issues were observed. So pass.

I'll output JSON.
